notes:
Five things, all originating from the v1.0.44 install on a clean machine looking wrong in small ways:

### Icon fixes

All Windows icon assets regenerated. v1.0.44 shipped icon.ico at 64KB with a stray "dot" artifact visible at small Taskbar / Alt-Tab sizes; v1.0.45's `icon.ico` is 592 bytes (single-resolution, clean rasterization) and the `32/64/128/128@2x/icon.png` PNGs were re-exported to match. Fixes the ghost dot in the Windows Taskbar.

### Window title rename: "ASA" → "ASA Trading Systems Terminal"

Across the board so the title bar matches the company name:

- `tray-app/src-tauri/tauri.conf.json` — `productName` and main-window `title` both updated.
- `tray-app/src-tauri/src/lib.rs` — dashboard `WebviewWindowBuilder` title updated.
- `tray-app/index.html` — `<title>` updated (the brief flash before Flask loads).
- `dashboard.html` — `<title>` updated (the title WebView2 reports for the dashboard window).

`mainBinaryName` stays `asa-terminal` and the bundle identifier stays `com.asasystems.terminal` — no installer / updater / autorun key churn.

### "Bridge" → "MT5 Connection" rename

The "Bridge" name was internal jargon (it's the broker bridge from a network-protocol POV) that didn't communicate to test users what the page does. Renamed in `dashboard.html` — sidebar nav button, view header, and the timeout/error alert copy that mentions opening "the Bridge tab again". Internal route IDs (`vbridge`, `tbridge`, `bridgeRefreshUpdateStatus`, etc.) intentionally NOT renamed; that's a much bigger refactor and the user-visible surface is what matters for test users.

### Update UI cleanup on the MT5 Connection page

The header `"ASA Terminal is up to date"` + the `"Auto-updates run hourly. You can also check now."` status line + the `Check now` button was a confused tri-state. Simplified to:

- Header always reads `ASA Terminal` (with version chip on the right).
- Auto-update checkbox added (persists to `localStorage` as `asa-auto-update`, default on); when off the user opts out of the hourly background tick.
- Status line is blank by default and only populates after an explicit `Check now` click — toast-style with 3-second fade (`bridgeShowUpdateStatus` + `_bridgeUpdateStatusTimer`).
- `no-update` result was a full `tsAlert` modal in v1.0.44; now it's a quiet inline `✓ You're on the latest version` instead. The modal interrupted the user every check; the toast doesn't.

### New wordmark + landing page

`tray-app/src/assets/wordmark-master-transparent.png` updated to the v2 master (new file under `branding/png/` for source-of-truth). `marketing/landing/` rewritten with the new wordmark, separated `scripts.js` / `styles.css`, plus an `assets/` and `branding/` folder. `serve_landing.bat` / `marketing/landing/start-server.bat` are local-dev convenience helpers for previewing the landing page.

### Files touched

- `tray-app/src-tauri/icons/{32x32,64x64,128x128,128x128@2x,icon}.png`, `tray-app/src-tauri/icons/icon.ico` — regenerated
- `tray-app/src-tauri/tauri.conf.json`, `tray-app/src-tauri/src/lib.rs`, `tray-app/index.html`, `dashboard.html` — title rename
- `dashboard.html` — Bridge → MT5 Connection rename + update-UI rework (`bridgeShowUpdateStatus`, `bridgeSaveAutoUpdate`, simplified `bridgeRefreshUpdateStatus`)
- `tray-app/src/assets/wordmark-master-transparent.png`, `branding/png/{wordmark-master-v2,wordmark-transparent,asa-systems-wordmark}.png` — new wordmark
- `marketing/landing/{index.html,scripts.js,styles.css,assets/,branding/asa-systems-wordmark.png,start-server.bat}` — landing-page rewrite
- `static/css/components.css` — minor polish carried over from v1.0.44 work
- `serve_landing.bat` — dev helper

### Auto-updater note

Same pipeline as v1.0.43 / v1.0.44. Manifest mirror-pushed to `cjml34483/ASA-releases` (canonical) and `cjml34483/TradeSpace-releases` (legacy stub for v1.0.30-v1.0.42 binaries).
